title: Atlas Academy Map Grade
description: A single, structured grading result for one map, per academy/grading-system.md. Combines nine per-category scores (schemas/academy-observation.schema.json and schemas/academy-map-metrics.schema.json are the raw evidence these scores must trace to), an overall recommendation, a final outcome drawn unchanged from studio/operations/PLAYTEST_AND_ACCEPTANCE.md's four-outcome vocabulary, and zero or more remediation tags giving that outcome actionable routing. A grade recommends; it does not itself accept or reject a build - per academy/grading-rubric.md, a Production Director's recorded PLAYTEST_AND_ACCEPTANCE.md decision remains the actual authority, exactly as the Work Order Router and Agent Scheduler recommend without dispatching.

schema_version.description: Version of this schema the record was produced against.

grade_id.description: Stable identifier for this grading pass, e.g. GRD-ASHFORDINN-001. A map graded more than once (a rejected pass, then a revised one) gets a new grade_id each time - grades are never overwritten in place, matching academy/compositions/README.md's per-pass filing convention.

ownership_state_at_capture.description: Matches schemas/academy-observation.schema.json's identical field - a grade is pinned to the map's ownership-ledger state at grading time, since that state can change later.

grader.description: The agent or human who produced this grade, matching the observer/owner convention used across every other Academy and pattern record.

category.description: Nine of work-orders/WO-2005-map-grading-system.md's ten named Example Categories. The tenth, 'overall recommendation', is not a tenth independently-measurable score - it is this record's own overall_recommendation field, a synthesis across the other nine, not a parallel axis. composition, traffic_flow, visual_hierarchy, and environmental_storytelling are also academy/composition-analysis.md topics; where both exist, this field's score is the composite verdict, and that document's finer-grained per-topic Holds/Gap/N/A detail is the evidence behind it, cited via this entry's evidence field rather than restated here.

score.description: 1 (fails this category badly) to 5 (exemplary), per academy/grading-rubric.md's scale - adapted from references/atlas_academy_jrpg_map_research.md's Part 10 proposed 1-5 rubric, at that document's own research-hypothesis confidence tier, not upgraded to a settled standard. Null only when confidence is insufficient_evidence - a category must never receive a numeric score invented in the absence of grounding evidence.

confidence.description: How well-grounded this category's score is, per studio/design-patterns/PATTERN_CONFIDENCE_MODEL.md's tiering spirit applied to a grading context. insufficient_evidence is a legitimate, expected value - not every category can be scored from what currently exists on file for a given target, and a grade recording that honestly is more useful than one that guesses.

rationale.description: The specific, falsifiable reason behind this score, per academy/curriculum.md Level 3's diagnostic standard - not a restatement of the score itself in words.

evidence.description: Paths to the observation record(s), metric record(s), composition analysis, reference source record, or other cited document(s) this score traces to. An empty array paired with confidence other than insufficient_evidence is invalid in spirit, though not schema-enforced as a hard dependency, matching this schema family's existing convention (see schemas/academy-observation.schema.json's inference_reasoning field).

summary.description: A short synthesis across all scored categories - not a restatement of each category, but the reasoning that connects them into one recommendation. WO-2005's tenth 'Example Category' lives here, not as a parallel score.

dragon_quest_exploration_feel_applicable.description: Whether the dragon_quest_exploration_feel category applied to this target at all, per WO-2005's own 'when applicable' qualifier - false for a target with no stated Dragon Quest-style exploration requirement (e.g. no governing DDR names it), in which case that category's score should be null with confidence insufficient_evidence or a note explaining inapplicability rather than a forced score.

outcome.description: Drawn unchanged from studio/operations/PLAYTEST_AND_ACCEPTANCE.md's four outcomes - this schema does not add a fifth or sixth top-level outcome value, per academy/grading-rubric.md's explicit reconciliation of WO-2005's six 'Required Outcomes' into four final outcomes plus two remediation tags (see remediation_tags). This field records what a grade recommends; the actual PLAYTEST_AND_ACCEPTANCE.md decision remains a separate, human, Production-Director act this schema does not perform or substitute for.

remediation_tags.description: Zero or more diagnostic routing tags, per academy/grading-rubric.md's reconciliation of WO-2005's Required Outcomes list. needs_art_direction routes to a composition/asset/tile-correctness follow-up (academy/composition-analysis.md, academy/observation-model.md's tile_usage category); needs_functional_fix routes to a passability/event/transfer follow-up (bridges/rpg-maker-mz/passability-rule.md). Both may apply to the same grade. An empty array is expected and correct for a clean accepted outcome.

notes.description: Free-text notes not captured by the structured fields above.
